jokes.text: FUCK. FUCKER. FUCK! AH!
jokes.setup: Larry is clearly struggling with opening vacuum-sealed packaging
jokes.punchline: His escalating profanity from the struggle
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective physical comedy setup for the packaging frustration theme

jokes.text: I AM SO SORRY THAT YOU HAD TO MAKE SUCH A LONG TREK UP HERE. I'M SITTING HERE SUFFERING WITH CANCER AND YOU HAD TO WALK UP SOME LITTLE STAIRS. SORRY ABOUT THAT, L.D.
jokes.setup: Larry complains about being called upstairs just to answer about the E! channel
jokes.punchline: Loretta sarcastically contrasts her cancer with his minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven sarcasm that establishes Loretta's personality and cancer card dynamic

jokes.text: I CAN'T OPEN THOSE PACKAGES. BAM! THERE IT IS.
jokes.setup: Larry cuts himself trying to open vacuum packaging
jokes.punchline: He immediately demonstrates the problem by cutting himself again
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that reinforces the episode's packaging theme

jokes.text: BUT HOW DOES IT AFFECT YOUR DRIVING?
jokes.setup: Loretta uses cancer as excuse not to drive
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but insensitive question about cancer's effect on driving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David logic - technically correct but socially tone-deaf

jokes.text: I KNOW WHAT IT FEELS LIKE TO BE WITH SOMEONE WHO HAS CANCER.
jokes.setup: Loretta asks if Larry knows what it feels like to have cancer
jokes.punchline: Larry's narcissistic response making it about himself
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Larry self-centeredness in the face of someone else's suffering

jokes.text: ANY BLACK PEOPLE GONNA BE THERE? - NO. - WHY WOULD I WANT TO GO?
jokes.setup: Larry invites Loretta to dinner
jokes.punchline: Her practical question and immediate disinterest when answered no
jokes.explanation: Economical character moment showing Loretta's direct approach to social situations

jokes.text: MIGHT BE SOME PEOPLE WITH CANCER.
jokes.setup: Loretta declines dinner because no black people will be there
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd counter-offer about cancer patients as company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating cancer patients as a demographic substitute for race

jokes.text: GOT A FINE-ASS WIFE. WIFE FINE AS HELL-- I DON'T KNOW WHY THE GUY SO DEPRESSED WITH A WIFE THAT DAMN FINE.
jokes.setup: Leon asks Larry to call his depressed friend
jokes.punchline: Leon's fixation on how attractive the friend's wife is
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Leon's blunt approach to discussing attraction

jokes.text: Well, someone who is impatient, someone who's obnoxious, someone who is petty and argumentative, and obsesses over meaningless details
jokes.setup: Dr. Trundle describes toxic spouse characteristics
jokes.punchline: She's perfectly describing Larry David
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the show describes its own protagonist as a toxic spouse

jokes.text: PHIL KREEL...TON.
jokes.setup: Loretta asks who recommended Dr. Trundle
jokes.punchline: Larry's obvious lie about 'Phil' being 'Phil Kreelton' instead of Dr. Phil
jokes.explanation: Good improvisational comedy showing Larry's terrible lying skills

jokes.text: I'M A FRIEND OF LEON'S. HE TOLD ME TO GIVE YOU A CALL. HE SAID IT WAS YOUR BIRTHDAY.
jokes.setup: Larry calls to cheer up Leon's depressed friend
jokes.punchline: This call will obviously make things much worse
jokes.explanation: Setup for the disaster to come - audience knows this won't end well

jokes.text: LEON SAID YOUR WIFE WAS BEAUTIFUL, SO I DON'T KNOW WHAT YOU HAVE TO BE DEPRESSED ABOUT
jokes.setup: Larry tries to cheer up Alton
jokes.punchline: He immediately makes it worse by mentioning Leon praising Alton's wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry disaster - trying to help but saying exactly the wrong thing

jokes.text: IT'S NOT GONNA LAST.
jokes.setup: Jeff points out Lewis's beautiful new girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate cynical assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's reflexive pessimism about relationships

jokes.text: SHE BLEW HIM IN THE CAR. BLOWJOB IN THE CAR? WHILE HE'S DRIVING.
jokes.setup: Jeff reveals Beverly gave Lewis road head
jokes.punchline: The escalating details make it more outrageous
jokes.explanation: Sets up the vehicular fellatio theme that will drive the episode

jokes.text: WOW, HOW GENTILE.
jokes.setup: Learning about the car blowjob
jokes.punchline: Larry's ethnic stereotype about sexual behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Larry's warped cultural observations

jokes.text: MORE DANGEROUS THAN CELL PHONES IN MY OPINION. ANY KIND OF JOB IS DANGEROUS.
jokes.setup: Discussion of car blowjobs
jokes.punchline: Larry's serious safety analysis of sexual acts while driving
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic treating sexual acts like a public safety issue

jokes.text: BY THE WAY, WHY DO THEY CALL IT A JOB? IS IT SO HARD? IS IT A LOT OF WORK?
jokes.setup: Discussion of sexual 'jobs'
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal interpretation of why oral sex is called a 'job'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observational humor about sexual terminology

jokes.text: IT'S A LOT OF WORK. I WOULDN'T WANT TO DO IT. IT IS A JOB. NO WONDER WHY THEY DON'T LIKE IT.
jokes.setup: Larry asks why oral sex is called a job
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact agreement that it's actual work
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the work metaphor with Jeff's practical assessment

jokes.text: WHERE HAVE YOU GUYS BEEN, HUH? WE'RE SITTING HERE FOR LIKE AN HOUR... THERE'S NO BREAD, THE SERVICE SUCKS.
jokes.setup: Beverly and Lewis finally join the table
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately complaining instead of greeting them
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness - complaining before pleasantries

jokes.text: I DON'T LIKE TO TAKE SIPS. I DON'T. I DON'T TAKE BITES EITHER. NO BITES, NO SIPS.
jokes.setup: Beverly insists Larry try her drink
jokes.punchline: Larry's rigid personal rules about food sharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - absurd but consistent personal boundaries

jokes.text: HE LIVES IN THE BOONDOCKS. I'LL NEVER FIND THE PLACE.
jokes.setup: Larry talking about Michael York's party
jokes.punchline: Sets up the GPS storyline about navigation problems
jokes.explanation: Basic setup for the packaging problem callback

jokes.text: WHO AM I, MOHAMED ATTA? I GOTTA BUY A BOX CUTTER?
jokes.setup: Susie suggests buying a box cutter to open packages
jokes.punchline: Larry's reference to the 9/11 hijacker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy connecting mundane packaging problems to terrorism

jokes.text: "PUT BUTTER UP MY ASS." I MEAN, IS THAT SUPPOSED TO BE A MOVIE?
jokes.setup: Discussing 'Last Tango in Paris'
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude reduction of the famous art film
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Larry's dismissal of pretentious art

jokes.text: SHE WANTED TO KISS YOU GOODBYE LIKE AN AUNT IN BROOKLYN AT A SEDER. YOU CAN'T EVEN DO THAT?
jokes.setup: Larry refused to kiss Beverly goodbye
jokes.punchline: Lewis's specific cultural reference to harmless familial kissing
jokes.explanation: Good cultural specificity making Larry's behavior seem more ridiculous

jokes.text: I HAVE A COLD SORE AND I DIDN'T WANT TO KISS HER. I WAS EMBARRASSED.
jokes.setup: Pressure to explain his behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake excuse that's actually socially acceptable
jokes.explanation: Sets up the miscommunication about the real reason

jokes.text: BUT I AM! I'M TAPPING THAT ASS! I'VE BEEN KUNG-FUING THAT ASS FOR A WHILE NOW.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests Leon just deny the affair
jokes.punchline: Leon admits he's actually having the affair using colorful language
jokes.explanation: Great character moment with Leon's unique expressions for sex

jokes.text: ASS IS ASS, LARRY. ASS IS FUCKING ASS, LARRY. IT'S WHAT THE FUCK I DO.
jokes.setup: Larry questions Leon's married woman policy
jokes.punchline: Leon's philosophy about sexual opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon character moment - crude but consistent philosophy

jokes.text: WE ARE LEGO. WE ARE A FUCKING LEGO SET RIGHT NOW. WE ARE FUCKING INTERLOCKED TOGETHER. WE FUCKED HER.
jokes.setup: Leon insists Larry is involved in his problem
jokes.punchline: Leon's bizarre Lego metaphor for shared culpability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor - using children's toys to describe complicity in adultery

jokes.text: IT'S LIKE HOW HORSES DO IT, RIGHT? AND I CAN SEE WHY THEY DO IT. IT FEELS GOOD.
jokes.setup: Larry interrupts the doctor's serious cancer discussion
jokes.punchline: His random observation about horse sexual behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate comment during serious moment

jokes.text: WHAT, I'M SUPPOSED TO HAVE A CONNECTION TO HER 'CAUSE I'M BLACK? DUH!
jokes.setup: Doctor points out African art in her office
jokes.punchline: Loretta's sarcastic response about racial assumptions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Loretta's directness about racial assumptions

jokes.text: A HAPPY AND A HEALTHY NEW YEAR TO YOU.
jokes.setup: Doctor asks about their 'happy, healthy' relationship
jokes.punchline: Larry mishears and responds with holiday greeting
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Larry's poor listening skills

jokes.text: OY, HE'S GOT SOME HEALTHY HEAD OF HAIR, THIS ONE. OH, HE WAS BLESSED. SO, WHAT? I'M UNBLESSED. IS THAT WHAT YOU'RE SAYING?
jokes.setup: Doctor mentions Loretta's husband's hair
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate insecurity about his baldness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry insecurity moment - making everything about himself

jokes.text: OH, CHRIST! THESE ARE-- ARE BROKEN. I'LL SEND YOU A BILL.
jokes.setup: Dean and Larry have an enthusiastic reunion hug
jokes.punchline: Dean's glasses break and he immediately demands payment
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the glasses liability dispute

jokes.text: I INSTIGATED THE HUG? I CAME IN FOR LIKE A SHAKE AND THEN I SAW YOU MAKE A MOVE FOR THE HUG, SO I WENT IN.
jokes.setup: Debate over who initiated the hug
jokes.punchline: Dean's detailed forensic analysis of the greeting interaction
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about analyzing a simple greeting

jokes.text: YOU OVERCOMPENSATED BY A SUPERSTRONG HUG THAT BROKE MY GLASSES.
jokes.setup: Dean's theory about Larry's hug motivation
jokes.punchline: His psychological analysis of Larry's overcompensation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the hug liability theory

jokes.text: FIRST OF ALL, I'M NOT A NEEDY PERSON. OKAY? THAT'S NUMBER ONE. I HAVE NO NEEDS AT ALL, OKAY?
jokes.setup: Dean calls Larry needy
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate, needy denial of being needy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic moment where Larry's protest proves the accusation

jokes.text: I'M GOING TO RIP IT UP INTO TINY LITTLE PIECES AND I MIGHT EVEN PEE ON IT.
jokes.setup: Dean threatens to send a bill
jokes.punchline: Larry's childish threat to destroy and urinate on the invoice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's petty vindictiveness

jokes.text: YOU KNOW I HAVE CANCER, RIGHT? YOU KNOW THAT'S WHY I'M HERE. I DON'T WANT TO HAVE A FIGHT WITH YOU. IN FACT, MY DOCTOR SAID TO NOT HAVE ANY FIGHTS AT ALL.
jokes.setup: The argument escalates
jokes.punchline: Dean pulls the cancer card to end the fight
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how cancer becomes the ultimate argument winner

jokes.text: PRETTY PRETTY PRETTY PRETTY GOOD.
jokes.setup: Larry's assessment of the doctor
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase response
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry catchphrase moment - recognizable but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: SOME PEOPLE ARE NOTHING EVEN WITH HEALTH. I FALL INTO THAT CATEGORY SOMETIMES. A NOTHING-- A BIG NOTHING-- AND I HAVE HEALTH.
jokes.setup: Larry talks about health being everything
jokes.punchline: His self-aware admission of being worthless despite good health
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character moment showing Larry's rare honest self-assessment

jokes.text: BUT JEFF TOLD ME THAT YOU GAVE LEWIS A BLOWJOB IN THE CAR BEFORE YOU GOT TO THE RESTAURANT, SO THAT'S WHY--
jokes.setup: Larry tries to apologize to Beverly
jokes.punchline: He immediately makes it worse by revealing the blowjob information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry disaster - trying to help but making everything catastrophically worse

jokes.text: I THOUGHT THE TRUTH WAS THAT YOU HAD A COLD SORE FOR CHRIST'S SAKE! THAT'S WHAT THE TRUTH WOULD HAVE BEEN!
jokes.setup: Larry said Lewis told him to tell the truth
jokes.punchline: Lewis clarifies he meant the fake cold sore truth, not the real truth
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedy about the relativity of truth and Larry's literalism

jokes.text: THAT'S THE SIGN FOR BLOWJOB. THAT'S A BLOWJOB SIGN. NO, IT'S COLD SORE. IT'S 'I'M CHOKING.' THAT'S COLD SORE?
jokes.setup: Argument about hand gestures
jokes.punchline: The confusion over what hand gesture means what
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy about gesture misinterpretation

jokes.text: SHE'S GOT YOUR SEMEN ON HER LIPS. I'M NOT GONNA KISS HER. YES! YEAH, SECONDHAND SEMEN.
jokes.setup: Lewis asks why Larry couldn't just kiss Beverly
jokes.punchline: Larry's graphic explanation about secondhand semen contact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant crude logic showing Larry's obsessive contamination fears

jokes.text: YOU CAN'T BE A HOMOSEXUAL BY PROXY. IT'S PROBABLY IN THE FUCKING CONSTITUTION. I THINK YOU CAN BE GAY BY PROXY.
jokes.setup: Discussion of secondhand sexual contact
jokes.punchline: Larry's legal/constitutional analysis of proxy homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic applying constitutional law to sexual anxieties

jokes.text: L.D., IS SHE GIVING HIM A BLOWJOB?! THAT'S WHAT IT LOOKS LIKE.
jokes.setup: They see Dr. Trundle in a car with her husband
jokes.punchline: The apparent vehicular fellatio sighting
jokes.explanation: Good callback bringing the episode theme full circle

jokes.text: I CAN'T TRUST A WOMAN LIKE THAT TO CURE MY CANCER, DOING SOMETHING LIKE THAT IN THE CAR!
jokes.setup: Seeing the apparent car blowjob
jokes.punchline: Loretta's logic connecting sexual behavior to medical competence
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic connecting unrelated professional and personal behavior

jokes.text: SHE MUST'VE BEEN DOING IT FOR THERAPEUTIC REASONS.
jokes.setup: Loretta rejects Dr. Trundle for the car incident
jokes.punchline: Larry's attempt to rationalize the doctor's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist rationalization attempting to medicalize sexual behavior

jokes.text: I WAS LOOKING FOR THE CELL PHONE. AN AWFUL LONG TIME TO LOOK FOR THE CELL PHONE.
jokes.setup: Dr. Trundle explains what Larry saw
jokes.punchline: Larry's skeptical response about the duration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's suspicion despite reasonable explanation

jokes.text: YES. YOU ARE A TINY, LITTLE, INSECURE, INFANTILE MIND OF ABOUT A 12-YEAR-OLD!
jokes.setup: Larry insists the doctor was performing oral sex
jokes.punchline: Dr. Trundle's psychological assessment of Larry
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier description of toxic spouse characteristics

jokes.text: I THINK YOU BLEW HIM. YOU THINK I BLEW HIM? YOU HAVE NOTHING BETTER IN YOUR MIND THAN THAT?!
jokes.setup: The argument reaches its peak
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt accusation and her outraged response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to the breaking point of the argument

jokes.text: GIVE YOUR ASS A LITTLE BITTY TASTE OF CHOCOLATE, AND NEXT THING YOU KNOW, YOU CHEATING ON ME?!
jokes.setup: Alton's wife confronts Larry about the affair
jokes.punchline: Her colorful description of Larry's alleged infidelity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with colorful language about racial dynamics

jokes.text: SHE WAS GIVING YOU A BLOWJOB?! OKAY, YEAH, WE KNOW WHAT IT IS. ALL RIGHT! ENOUGH!
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Jeff and Beverly stuck after car sex
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable confirmation and attempt to stop discussing it
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff to the episode's central theme

jokes.text: I TOLD YOU THOSE BLOWJOBS WERE DANGEROUS.
jokes.setup: The dangerous car sex situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's vindication of his earlier safety concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback moment where Larry's earlier absurd safety analysis is proven correct
